note: Feminine form is 'douce'. Use 'pelage doux' to describe a soft coat.
Affectionate way to describe a large, chill pet. Feminine is 'grosse mémère'.
The French just say 'faire le popcorn' for that joyful guinea pig jump — delightful
The standard French word for dry pet food — used for cats and dogs
Used for cat litter boxes and small animal bedding alike
The Quebec French word — France uses 'moufette'
The Quebec French word — France uses 'élan'
In Québec, you might hear 'minou' as a cute nickname for a cat.

fr: Le chien a besoin d'une promenade.
ph: luh SHYAN ah buh-ZWAN dyoon prohm-NAD

fr: Il faut aller chez le vétérinaire.
en: We need to go to the vet.
ph: eel foh ah-LAY shay luh vay-tay-ree-NAIR

note: Used when an animal is impressively large or sturdy.

en: to go to bed / lie down

ph: meel nuf sahn kah-truh van WEET

en: to hang around / to fool around (QC)

fr: On a eu une belle journée
